text: The surgeon initiates the intersphincteric resection using a total mesorectal excision (TME) approach, focusing on precise dissection within the pelvic cavity.

The surgeon performs intersphincterial resection, carefully removing partial or total internal anal sphincter muscles while preserving the external sphincter muscles, ensuring no visible injury to surrounding structures.

Following the completion of the intersphincteric resection, the surgeon divides the rectum using an endogia stapler, visibly separating the rectal tissue.

The surgeon performs a pelvic lymph node dissection, resecting lymph nodes and fatty tissues in the area surrounding the common iliac vessels.

The surgeon dissects the lateral lymph nodes surrounding the external iliac artery, internal iliac artery, and obturator fossa. The left ureter is visible crossing in front of the bifurcation of the common iliac artery, and it is carefully identified and mobilized to prevent injury.
